'registered', true);\n233\t\n234\t            $approverInfo = $this->getApproverInfo($authUser);\n235\t            $emailSent = $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification($user, 'approved', $approverInfo['display_name']);\n236\t            if (!$emailSent) {\n237\t                Log::warning('Approval email failed to send', [\n238\t                    'user_id' => $user->userID,\n239\t                    'email' => $user->email,\n240\t                    'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n241\t                    'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n242\t                ]);\n243\t\n244\t                return response()->json([\n245\t                    'message' => 'User approved, but approval email failed to send.',\n246\t                    'user' => $user,\n247\t                    'email' => $user->email,\n248\t                    'approval_info' => $approverInfo,\n249\t                ], 200);\n250\t            }\n251\t\n252\t            Log::info('User approved successfully', [\n253\t                'user_id' => $user->userID,\n254\t                'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n255\t                'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n256\t                'approver_scope' => $approverInfo['scope'],\n257\t            ]);\n258\t\n259\t            return response()->json([\n260\t                'message' => 'User approved successfully.',\n261\t                'user' => $user,\n262\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n263\t            ], 200);\n264\t\n265\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n266\t            Log::error('User approval error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n267\t            return response()->json([\n268\t                'message' => 'An error occurred while approving the user.',\n269\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n270\t            ], 500);\n271\t        }\n272\t    }\n273\t\n274\t    /**\n275\t     * Check if the authenticated user can approve the target user based on role hierarchy\n276\t     */\n277\t    private function canApproveUser($authUser, $targetUser)\n278\t    {\n279\t        // Dean can approve anyone\n280\t        if ($authUser->roleID === 4) {\n281\t            return true;\n282\t        }\n283\t\n284\t        // Get allowed roles for approval based on auth user's role\n285\t        $allowedRoles = $this->getAllowedApprovalRoles($authUser->roleID);\n286\t\n287\t        // Check if target user's role is in the allowed roles\n288\t        return in_array($targetUser->roleID, $allowedRoles);\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /**\n292\t     * Get the list of roles that can be approved by a given role\n293\t     */\n294\t    private function getAllowedApprovalRoles($roleID)\n295\t    {\n296\t        return match($roleID) {\n297\t            4 => [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], // Dean can approve all\n298\t            5 => [1, 2, 3],      // Associate Dean can approve Program Chair, Instructor, and Student\n299\t            3 => [1, 2],         // Program Chair can approve Instructor and Student\n300\t            2 => [1],            // Faculty can approve Student\n301\t            default => []\n302\t        };\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /**\n306\t     * Disapprove user (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n307\t     * Scope restriction: Associate Deans can only disapprove within their campus,\n308\t     * but Program Chairs and Faculty cannot disapprove (only Dean/Associate Dean can).\n309\t     */\n310\t    public function disapproveUser(Request $request, $userID)\n311\t    {\n312\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n313\t        \n314\t        // Only Dean (4) and Associate Dean (5) can disapprove\n315\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n316\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only Dean or Associate Dean can disapprove users.'], 403);\n317\t        }\n318\t\n319\t        $user = User::findOrFail($userID);\n320\t\n321\t        // Associate Dean scope check: can only disapprove users in their campus\n322\t        if ($authUser->roleID === 5 && $user->campusID !== $authUser->campusID) {\n323\t            return response()->json([\n324\t                'message' => 'You can only disapprove users within your assigned campus.'\n325\t            ], 403);\n326\t        }\n327\t\n328\t        $this->updateUserStatus($user, 'disapproved', false);\n329\t\n330\t        $approverInfo = $this->getApproverInfo($authUser);\n331\t        $emailSent = $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification($user, 'disapproved', $approverInfo['display_name']);\n332\t        if (!$emailSent) {\n333\t            Log::warning('Disapproval email failed to send', [\n334\t                'user_id' => $user->userID,\n335\t                'email' => $user->email,\n336\t                'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n337\t                'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n338\t            ]);\n339\t\n340\t            return response()->json([\n341\t                'message' => 'User disapproved, but disapproval email failed to send.',\n342\t                'user' => $user,\n343\t                'email' => $user->email,\n344\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo,\n345\t            ], 200);\n346\t        }\n347\t\n348\t        Log::info('User disapproved', [\n349\t            'user_id' => $user->userID,\n350\t            'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n351\t            'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n352\t        ]);\n353\t\n354\t        return response()->json([\n355\t            'message' => 'User has been disapproved.',\n356\t            'user' => $user,\n357\t            'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n358\t        ], 200);\n359\t    }\n360\t\n361\t    /**\n362\t     * Approve multiple users at once with role-based scope validation.\n363\t     * Dean can approve all, Associate Dean within their campus,\n364\t     * Program Chair within their program, Faculty for their students.\n365\t     */\n366\t    public function approveMultipleUsers(Request $request)\n367\t    {\n368\t        try {\n369\t            $authUser = Auth::user();\n370\t            \n371\t            // Only Dean, Associate Dean, Program Chair, and Faculty can bulk approve\n372\t            if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [2, 3, 4, 5])) {\n373\t                return response()->json([\n374\t                    'message' => 'Unauthorized: You do not have permission to approve users.'\n375\t                ], 403);\n376\t            }\n377\t\n378\t            $validated = $this->validateUserIDs($request);\n379\t            $statusIds = $this->getStatusIds();\n380\t            $approverInfo = $this->getApproverInfo($authUser);\n381\t            \n382\t            $results = $this->processMultipleApprovalsWithScope(\n383\t                $validated['userIDs'],\n384\t                $statusIds,\n385\t                $authUser,\n386\t                $approverInfo\n387\t            );\n388\t\n389\t            return response()->json([\n390\t                'message' => 'Bulk approval completed.',\n391\t                'approved_users' => $results['approved'],\n392\t                'skipped_users' => $results['skipped'],\n393\t                'scope_restricted_users' => $results['scope_restricted'],\n394\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n395\t            ], 200);\n396\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n397\t            Log::error('Bulk approval error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n398\t            return response()->json([\n399\t                'message' => 'An error occurred during bulk approval.',\n400\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n401\t            ], 500);\n402\t        }\n403\t    }\n404\t\n405\t    /**\n406\t     * Update the authenticated user's profile.\n407\t     */\n408\t    public function updateProfile(Request $request)\n409\t    {\n410\t        try {\n411\t            $user = Auth::user();\n412\t            if (!$user) {\n413\t                return response()->json(['message' => 'User not authenticated.'], 401);\n414\t            }\n415\t\n416\t            $validated = $this->validateProfileUpdate($request, $user);\n417\t            $this->updateUserProfile($user, $validated);\n418\t\n419\t            return response()->json([\n420\t                'message' => 'Profile updated successfully.',\n421\t                'user' => $user\n422\t            ]);\n423\t        } catch (\\Illuminate\\Validation\\ValidationException $e) {\n424\t            return $this->handleValidationError($e);\n425\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n426\t            Log::error('Profile update error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n427\t            return response()->json([\n428\t                'message' => 'An unexpected error occurred while updating your profile.',\n429\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n430\t            ], 500);\n431\t        }\n432\t    }\n433\t\n434\t    /**\n435\t     * Change user role with specific permissions.\n436\t     */\n437\t    public function changeUserRole(Request $request, $userID)\n438\t    {\n439\t        try {\n440\t            $authUser = Auth::user();\n441\t            if (!$authUser) {\n442\t                return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthenticated'], 401);\n443\t            }\n444\t\n445\t            // Validate role change permission\n446\t            if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [3, 4, 5])) {\n447\t                return response()->json([\n448\t                    'message' => 'Unauthorized: Only Dean, Associate Dean, or Program Chair can change user roles'\n449\t                ], 403);\n450\t            }\n451\t\n452\t            // Validate request data\n453\t            $validated = $request->validate([\n454\t                'roleID' => 'required|integer|exists:roles,roleID'\n455\t            ]);\n456\t\n457\t            // Get target user\n458\t            $user = User::findOrFail($userID);\n459\t\n460\t            // Role hierarchy restrictions\n461\t            if ($authUser->roleID === 3) { // Program Chair\n462\t                // Program Chair cannot edit Associate Dean (5) or Dean (4)\n463\t                if ($user->roleID >= 4) {\n464\t                    return response()->json([\n465\t                        'message' => 'Program Chair cannot modify roles of Associate Dean or Dean'\n466\t                    ], 403);\n467\t                }\n468\t                // Can only edit users in their program\n469\t                if ($user->programID !== $authUser->programID) {\n470\t                    return response()->json([\n471\t                        'message' => 'You can only change roles of users within your program'\n472\t                    ], 403);\n473\t                }\n474\t            } elseif ($authUser->roleID === 5) { // Associate Dean\n475\t                // Associate Dean cannot edit Dean (4)\n476\t                if ($user->roleID === 4) {\n477\t                    return response()->json([\n478\t                        'message' => 'Associate Dean cannot modify Dean\\'s role'\n479\t                    ], 403);\n480\t                }\n481\t                // Can only edit users in their campus\n482\t                if ($user->campusID !== $authUser->campusID) {\n483\t                    return response()->json([\n484\t                        'message' => 'You can only change roles of users within your campus'\n485\t                    ], 403);\n486\t                }\n487\t            }\n488\t            // Dean (roleID 4) can edit everyone, no restrictions needed\n489\t\n490\t            // Validate role change scope\n491\t            $allowedRoleChanges = $this->getAllowedRoleChanges($authUser->roleID);\n492\t            if (!in_array($validated['roleID'], $allowedRoleChanges)) {\n493\t                return response()->json([\n494\t                    'message' => 'You are not authorized to assign this role'\n495\t                ], 403);\n496\t            }\n497\t\n498\t            // Update the role\n499\t            $user->roleID = $validated['roleID'];\n500\t            $user->save();\n501\t\n502\t            return response()->json([\n503\t                'message' => 'User role updated successfully',\n504\t                'user' => $user\n505\t            ], 200);\n506\t\n507\t        } catch (\\Illuminate\\Validation\\ValidationException $e) {\n508\t            return response()->json([\n509\t                'message' => 'Validation failed',\n510\t                'errors' => $e->errors()\n511\t            ], 422);\n512\t        } catch (\\Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\ModelNotFoundException $e) {\n513\t            return response()->json([\n514\t                'message' => 'User not found'\n515\t            ], 404);\n516\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n517\t            Log::error('Role change error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n518\t            return response()->json([\n519\t                'message' => 'An unexpected error occurred while changing the user role',\n520\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n521\t            ], 500);\n522\t        }\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    /**\n526\t     * Delete a specific user (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n527\t     */\n528\t    public function deleteUser($id)\n529\t    {\n530\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n531\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n532\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can delete users'], 403);\n533\t        }\n534\t        $user = User::find($id);\n535\t        if (!$user) {\n536\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'User not found'], 404);\n537\t        }\n538\t        $user->delete();\n539\t        return response()->json(['message' => 'User deleted successfully'], 200);\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /**\n543\t     * Delete multiple users (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n544\t     */\n545\t    public function deleteMultipleUsers(Request $request)\n546\t    {\n547\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n548\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n549\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can delete users'], 403);\n550\t        }\n551\t        $validated = $request->validate([\n552\t            'userIDs' => 'required|array',\n553\t            'userIDs.*' => 'integer|exists:users,userID'\n554\t        ]);\n555\t        $deleted = [];\n556\t        foreach ($validated['userIDs'] as $userID) {\n557\t            $user = User::find($userID);\n558\t            if ($user) {\n559\t                $user->delete();\n560\t                $deleted[] = $userID;\n561\t            }\n562\t        }\n563\t        return response()->json([\n564\t            'message' => 'Selected users deleted successfully.',\n565\t            'deleted_users' => $deleted\n566\t        ], 200);\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    // Private helper methods\n570\t\n571\t    private function authorizeDeanAccess()\n572\t    {\n573\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n574\t        abort_unless($authUser && in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5]), 403, 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can perform this action');\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    private function buildUserQuery(Request $request)\n578\t    {\n579\t        $query = User::with(['role', 'campus', 'program', 'status', 'student']);\n580\t        $user = Auth::user();\n581\t\n582\t        // Apply role-based filters\n583\t        if ($user->roleID === 5) {\n584\t            // Associate Dean can only view users from their campus\n585\t            if ($user->campusID) {\n586\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID);\n587\t            } else {\n588\t                Log::warning(\"Associate Dean {$user->userID} has no campus assigned\");\n589\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n590\t            }\n591\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 3) {\n592\t            // Program Chair can only view users from their campus and program\n593\t            if ($user->campusID && $user->programID) {\n594\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID)\n595\t                      ->where('programID', $user->programID);\n596\t            } else {\n597\t                Log::warning(\"Program Chair {$user->userID} has missing campus or program assignment\");\n598\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n599\t            }\n600\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 2) {\n601\t            // Faculty can only view students from their campus and program\n602\t            if ($user->campusID && $user->programID) {\n603\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID)\n604\t                      ->where('programID', $user->programID)\n605\t                      ->where('roleID', 1); // Only show students (roleID 1)\n606\t            } else {\n607\t                Log::warning(\"Faculty {$user->userID} has missing campus or program assignment\");\n608\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n609\t            }\n610\t        }\n611\t        // Dean (roleID 4) can view all users, so no additional filters needed\n612\t\n613\t        $this->applyDefaultStateVisibility($query, $request);\n614\t        $this->applySearchFilters($query, $request);\n615\t        return $query;\n616\t    }\n617\t\n618\t    private function applyDefaultStateVisibility($query, Request $request)\n619\t    {\n620\t        $state = strtolower(trim((string) $request->input('state', '')));\n621\t        $status = strtolower(trim((string) $request->input('status', '')));\n622\t\n623\t        // If state or status is explicitly provided, use that filter\n624\t        // Also return early for non-default statuses so they bypass the default registered-only filter\n625\t        if (in_array($state, ['active', 'inactive', 'all'], true)\n626\t            || in_array($status, ['pending', 'registered', 'disapproved'], true)) {\n627\t            return;\n628\t        }\n629\t\n630\t        // Default: show only active registered users\n631\t        $query->where(function ($q) {\n632\t            $q->where('isActive', true)\n633\t              ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n634\t                  $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n635\t              });\n636\t        });\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    private function applySearchFilters($query, Request $request)\n640\t    {\n641\t        $filters = [\n642\t            'search' => function($q, $value) {\n643\t                $q->where(function($q) use ($value) {\n644\t                    $q->where('firstName', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n645\t                      ->orWhere('lastName', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n646\t                      ->orWhere('email', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n647\t                      ->orWhere('userCode', 'like', \"%{$value}%\");\n648\t                });\n649\t            },\n650\t            'status' => function($q, $value) {\n651\t                if ($value && $value !== 'all') {\n652\t                    $q->whereHas('status', function($q) use ($value) {\n653\t                        $q->where('name', $value);\n654\t                    });\n655\t                }\n656\t            },\n657\t            'campus' => function($q, $value) {\n658\t                $q->whereHas('campus', function($q) use ($value) {\n659\t                    $q->where('campusName', $value);\n660\t                });\n661\t            },\n662\t            'role' => function($q, $value) {\n663\t                $q->whereHas('role', function($q) use ($value) {\n664\t                    $q->where('roleName', $value);\n665\t                });\n666\t            },\n667\t            'position' => function($q, $value) {\n668\t                $q->whereHas('role', function($q) use ($value) {\n669\t                    $q->where('roleName', $value);\n670\t                });\n671\t            },\n672\t            'program' => function($q, $value) {\n673\t                $q->whereHas('program', function($q) use ($value) {\n674\t                    $q->where('programName', $value);\n675\t                });\n676\t            },\n677\t            'state' => function($q, $value) {\n678\t                $normalizedValue = strtolower(trim((string) $value));\n679\t\n680\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'all' || $normalizedValue === '') {\n681\t                    return;\n682\t                }\n683\t\n684\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'active') {\n685\t                    $q->where('isActive', true)\n686\t                      ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n687\t                          $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n688\t                      });\n689\t                    return;\n690\t                }\n691\t\n692\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'inactive') {\n693\t                    $q->where('isActive', false)\n694\t                      ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n695\t                          $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n696\t                      });\n697\t                }\n698\t            }\n699\t        ];\n700\t\n701\t        foreach ($filters as $key => $callback) {\n702\t            if ($value = $request->input($key)) {\n703\t                $callback($query, $value);\n704\t            }\n705\t        }\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    private function paginateResults($query, Request $request)\n709\t    {\n710\t        $perPage = min((int)$request->input('limit', 20), 100); // Default 20, max 100\n711\t        $page = max((int)$request->input('page', 1), 1);\n712\t        $total = $query->count();\n713\t\n714\t        $users = $query->orderBy('userID', 'desc')\n715\t                      ->skip(($page - 1) * $perPage)\n716\t                      ->take($perPage)\n717\t                      ->get()\n718\t                      ->map(function ($user) {\n719\t                          // Fetch remarks and curriculum for students\n720\t                          $remarks = null;\n721\t                          $curriculum = null;\n722\t                          if ($user->roleID == 1) {\n723\t                              $remarksRow = \\DB::table('student_remarks')\n724\t                                  ->join('remarks', 'student_remarks.remarksID', '=', 'remarks.id')\n725\t                                  ->where('student_remarks.userID', $user->userID)\n726\t                                  ->select('remarks.remarksType')\n727\t                                  ->first();\n728\t                              $remarks = $remarksRow ? $remarksRow->remarksType : null;\n729\t                              $curriculumRow = \\DB::table('student_curricula')\n730\t                                  ->join('curriculum', 'student_curricula.curriculumID', '=', 'curriculum.id')\n731\t                                  ->where('student_curricula.userID', $user->userID)\n732\t                                  ->select('curriculum.curriculumType')\n733\t                                  ->first();\n734\t                              $curriculum = $curriculumRow ? $curriculumRow->curriculumType : null;\n735\t                          }\n736\t                          return [\n737\t                              'userID' => $user->userID,\n738\t                              'userCode' => $user->userCode,\n739\t                              'firstName' => $user->firstName,\n740\t                              'lastName' => $user->lastName,\n741\t                              'email' => $user->email,\n742\t                              'roleID' => $user->roleID,\n743\t                              'campusID' => $user->campusID,\n744\t                              'programID' => $user->programID,\n745\t                              'role' => $user->role ? $user->role->roleName : 'Unknown',\n746\t                              'campus' => $user->campus ? $user->campus->campusName : 'Unknown',\n747\t                              'program' => $user->program ? $user->program->programName : 'Not Assigned',\n748\t                              'isActive' => $user->isActive,\n749\t                              'status_id' => $user->status_id,\n750\t                              'status' => $user->status ? $user->status->name : 'Unknown',\n751\t                              'remarks' => $remarks,\n752\t                              'curriculum' => $curriculum,\n753\t                              'yearLevel' => $user->student ? $user->student->yearLevel : null,\n754\t                              'student_profile' => $user->student ? [\n755\t                                  'year_level' => $user->student->yearLevel,\n756\t                                  'block' => $user->student->block,\n757\t                                  'programID' => $user->student->programID,\n758\t                              ] : null,\n759\t                          ];\n760\t                      });\n761\t\n762\t        return [\n763\t            'users' => $users,\n764\t            'total' => $total,\n765\t            'page' => (int)$page,\n766\t            'totalPages' => ceil($total / $perPage)\n767\t        ];\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    private function canUpdateUser($authUser, $userId)\n771\t    {\n772\t        return $authUser->roleID < 3 || $authUser->userID == $userId;\n773\t    }\n774\t\n775\t    private function validateUserIDs(Request $request)\n776\t    {\n777\t        return $request->validate([\n778\t            'userIDs' => 'required|array',\n779\t            'userIDs.*' => 'integer|exists:users,userID'\n780\t        ]);\n781\t    }\n782\t\n783\t    private function processMultipleUsers($userIDs, $activate)\n784\t    {\n785\t        $processedUsers = [];\n786\t                $user->save();\n791\t                $processedUsers[] = $userID;\n792\t            }\n793\t        }\n794\t        return $processedUsers;\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    private function           ], 403);\n496\t            }\n497\t\n498\t            // Update the role\n499\t            $user->roleID = $validated['roleID'];\n500\t            $user->save();\n501\t\n502\t            return response()->json([\n503\t                'message' => 'User role updated successfully',\n504\t                'user' => $user\n505\t            ], 200);\n506\t\n507\t        } catch (\\Illuminate\\Validation\\ValidationException $e) {\n508\t            return response()->json([\n509\t                'message' => 'Validation failed',\n510\t                'errors' => $e->errors()\n511\t            ], 422);\n512\t        } catch (\\Illuminate\\Database\\Eloquent\\ModelNotFoundException $e) {\n513\t            return response()->json([\n514\t                'message' => 'User not found'\n515\t            ], 404);\n516\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n517\t            Log::error('Role change error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n518\t            return response()->json([\n519\t                'message' => 'An unexpected error occurred while changing the user role',\n520\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n521\t            ], 500);\n522\t        }\n523\t    }\n524\t\n525\t    /**\n526\t     * Delete a specific user (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n527\t     */\n528\t    public function deleteUser($id)\n529\t    {\n530\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n531\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n532\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can delete users'], 403);\n533\t        }\n534\t        $user = User::find($id);\n535\t        if (!$user) {\n536\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'User not found'], 404);\n537\t        }\n538\t        $user->delete();\n539\t        return response()->json(['message' => 'User deleted successfully'], 200);\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    /**\n543\t     * Delete multiple users (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n544\t     */\n545\t    public function deleteMultipleUsers(Request $request)\n546\t    {\n547\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n548\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n549\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can delete users'], 403);\n550\t        }\n551\t        $validated = $request->validate([\n552\t            'userIDs' => 'required|array',\n553\t            'userIDs.*' => 'integer|exists:users,userID'\n554\t        ]);\n555\t        $deleted = [];\n556\t $userID;\n561\t            }\n562\t        }\n563\t        return response()->json([\n564\t            'message' => 'Selected users deleted successfully.',\n565\t            'deleted_users' => $deleted\n566\t        ], 200);\n567\t    }\n568\t\n569\t    // Private helper methods\n570\t\n571\t    private function authorizeDeanAccess()\n572\t    {\n573\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n574\t        abort_unless($authUser && in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5]), 403, 'Unauthorized: Only the Dean or Associate Dean can perform this action');\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    private function buildUserQuery(Request $request)\n578\t    {\n579\t        $query = User::with(['role', 'campus', 'program', 'status', 'student']);\n580\t        $user = Auth::user();\n581\t\n582\t        // Apply role-based filters\n583\t        if ($user->roleID === 5) {\n584\t            // Associate Dean can only view users from their campus\n585\t            if ($user->campusID) {\n586\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID);\n587\t            } else {\n588\t                Log::warning(\"Associate Dean {$user->userID} has no campus assigned\");\n589\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n590\t            }\n591\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 3) {\n592\t            // Program Chair can only view users from their campus and program\n593\t            if ($user->campusID && $user->programID) {\n594\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID)\n595\t                      ->where('programID', $user->programID);\n596\t            } else {\n597\t                Log::warning(\"Program Chair {$user->userID} has missing campus or program assignment\");\n598\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n599\t            }\n600\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 2) {\n601\t            // Faculty can only view students from their campus and program\n602\t            if ($user->campusID && $user->programID) {\n603\t                $query->where('campusID', $user->campusID)\n604\t                      ->where('programID', $user->programID)\n605\t                      ->where('roleID', 1); // Only show students (roleID 1)\n606\t            } else {\n607\t                Log::warning(\"Faculty {$user->userID} has missing campus or program assignment\");\n608\t                $query->where('campusID', 0); // This will return no results\n609\t            }\n610\t        }\n611\t        // Dean (roleID 4) can view all users, so no additional filters needed\n612\t\n613\t        $this->applyDefaultStateVisibility($query, $request);\n614\t        $this->applySearchFilters($query, $request);\n615\t        return $query;\n616\t    }\n617\t\n618\t    private function applyDefaultStateVisibility($query, Request $request)\n619\t    {\n620\t        $state = strtolower(trim((string) $request->input('state', '')));\n621\t        $status = strtolower(trim((string) $request->input('status', '')));\n622\t\n623\t        // If state or status is explicitly provided, use that filter\n624\t        // Also return early for non-default statuses so they bypass the default registered-only filter\n625\t        if (in_array($state, ['active', 'inactive', 'all'], true)\n626\t            || in_array($status, ['pending', 'registered', 'disapproved'], true)) {\n627\t            return;\n628\t        }\n629\t\n630\t        // Default: show only active registered users\n631\t        $query->where(function ($q) {\n632\t            $q->where('isActive', true)\n633\t              ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n634\t                  $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n635\t              });\n636\t        });\n637\t    }\n638\t\n639\t    private function applySearchFilters($query, Request $request)\n640\t    {\n641\t        $filters = [\n642\t            'search' => function($q, $value) {\n643\t                $q->where(function($q) use ($value) {\n644\t                    $q->where('firstName', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n645\t                      ->orWhere('lastName', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n646\t                      ->orWhere('email', 'like', \"%{$value}%\")\n647\t                      ->orWhere('userCode', 'like', \"%{$value}%\");\n648\t                });\n649\t            },\n650\t            'status' => function($q, $value) {\n651\t                if ($value && $value !== 'all') {\n652\t                    $q->whereHas('status', function($q) use ($value) {\n653\t                        $q->where('name', $value);\n654\t                    });\n655\t                }\n656\t            },\n657\t            'campus' => function($q, $value) {\n658\t                $q->whereHas('campus', function($q) use ($value) {\n659\t                    $q->where('campusName', $value);\n660\t                });\n661\t            },\n662\t            'role' => function($q, $value) {\n663\t                $q->whereHas('role', function($q) use ($value) {\n664\t                    $q->where('roleName', $value);\n665\t                });\n666\t            },\n667\t            'position' => function($q, $value) {\n668\t                $q->whereHas('role', function($q) use ($value) {\n669\t                    $q->where('roleName', $value);\n670\t                });\n671\t            },\n672\t            'program' => function($q, $value) {\n673\t                $q->whereHas('program', function($q) use ($value) {\n674\t                    $q->where('programName', $value);\n675\t                });\n676\t            },\n677\t            'state' => function($q, $value) {\n678\t                $normalizedValue = strtolower(trim((string) $value));\n679\t\n680\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'all' || $normalizedValue === '') {\n681\t                    return;\n682\t                }\n683\t\n684\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'active') {\n685\t                    $q->where('isActive', true)\n686\t                      ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n687\t                          $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n688\t                      });\n689\t                    return;\n690\t                }\n691\t\n692\t                if ($normalizedValue === 'inactive') {\n693\t                    $q->where('isActive', false)\n694\t                      ->whereHas('status', function ($statusQuery) {\n695\t                          $statusQuery->where('name', 'registered');\n696\t                      });\n697\t                }\n698\t            }\n699\t        ];\n700\t\n701\t        foreach ($filters as $key => $callback) {\n702\t            if ($value = $request->input($key)) {\n703\t                $callback($query, $value);\n704\t            }\n705\t        }\n706\t    }\n707\t\n708\t    private function paginateResults($query, Request $request)\n709\t    {\n710\t        $perPage = min((int)$request->input('limit', 20), 100); // Default 20, max 100\n711\t        $page = max((int)$request->input('page', 1), 1);\n712\t        $total = $query->count();\n713\t\n714\t        $users = $query->orderBy('userID', 'desc')\n715\t                      ->skip(($page - 1) * $perPage)\n716\t                      ->take($perPage)\n717\t                      ->get()\n718\t                      ->map(function ($user) {\n719\t                          // Fetch remarks and curriculum for students\n720\t                          $remarks = null;\n721\t                          $curriculum = null;\n722\t                          if ($user->roleID == 1) {\n723\t                              $remarksRow = \\DB::table('student_remarks')\n724\t                                  ->join('remarks', 'student_remarks.remarksID', '=', 'remarks.id')\n725\t                                  ->where('student_remarks.userID', $user->userID)\n726\t                                  ->select('remarks.remarksType')\n727\t                                  ->first();\n728\t                              $remarks = $remarksRow ? $remarksRow->remarksType : null;\n729\t                              $curriculumRow = \\DB::table('student_curricula')\n730\t                                  ->join('curriculum', 'student_curricula.curriculumID', '=', 'curriculum.id')\n731\t                                  ->where('student_curricula.userID', $user->userID)\n732\t                                  ->select('curriculum.curriculumType')\n733\t                                  ->first();\n734\t                              $curriculum = $curriculumRow ? $curriculumRow->curriculumType : null;\n735\t                          }\n736\t                          return [\n737\t                              'userID' => $user->userID,\n738\t                              'userCode' => $user->userCode,\n739\t                              'firstName' => $user->firstName,\n740\t                              'lastName' => $user->lastName,\n741\t                              'email' => $user->email,\n742\t                              'roleID' => $user->roleID,\n743\t                              'campusID' => $user->campusID,\n744\t                              'programID' => $user->programID,\n745\t                              'role' => $user->role ? $user->role->roleName : 'Unknown',\n746\t                              'campus' => $user->campus ? $user->campus->campusName : 'Unknown',\n747\t                              'program' => $user->program ? $user->program->programName : 'Not Assigned',\n748\t                              'isActive' => $user->isActive,\n749\t                              'status_id' => $user->status_id,\n750\t                              'status' => $user->status ? $user->status->name : 'Unknown',\n751\t                              'remarks' => $remarks,\n752\t                              'curriculum' => $curriculum,\n753\t                              'yearLevel' => $user->student ? $user->student->yearLevel : null,\n754\t                              'student_profile' => $user->student ? [\n755\t                                  'year_level' => $user->student->yearLevel,\n756\t                                  'block' => $user->student->block,\n757\t                                  'programID' => $user->student->programID,\n758\t                              ] : null,\n759\t                          ];\n760\t                      });\n761\t\n762\t        return [\n763\t            'users' => $users,\n764\t            'total' => $total,\n765\t            'page' => (int)$page,\n766\t            'totalPages' => ceil($total / $perPage)\n767\t        ];\n768\t    }\n769\t\n770\t    private function canUpdateUser($authUser, $userId)\n771\t    {\n772\t        return $authUser->roleID < 3 || $authUser->userID == $userId;\n773\t    }\n774\t\n775\t    private function validateUserIDs(Request $request)\n776\t    {\n777\t        return $request->validate([\n778\t            'userIDs' => 'required|array',\n779\t            'userIDs.*' => 'integer|exists:users,userID'\n780\t        ]);\n781\t    }\n782\t\n783\t    private function processMultipleUsers($userIDs, $activate)\n784\t    {\n785\t        $processedUsers = [];\n786\t                $user->save();\n791\t                $processedUsers[] = $userID;\n792\t            }\n793\t        }\n794\t        return $processedUsers;\n795\t    }\n796\t\n797\t    private function isUserPending($user)\n798\t    {\n799\t        $pendingStatusId = DB::table('statuses')->where('name', 'pending')->first()->id;\n800\t        return $user->status_id === $pendingStatusId;\n801\t    }\n802\t\n803\t    private function updateUserStatus($user, $status, $isActive)\n804\t    {\n805\t        $statusId = DB::table('statuses')->where('name', $status)->first()->id;\n806\t        $user->update([\n807\t            'status_id' => $statusId,\n808\t            'isActive' => $isActive\n809\t        ]);\n810\t    }\n811\t\n812\t    private function getStatusIds()\n813\t    {\n814\t        return [\n815\t            'pending' => DB::table('statuses')->where('name', 'pending')->first()->id,\n816\t            'registered' => DB::table('statuses')->where('name', processMultipleApprovals($userIDs, $statusIds, $approverName = 'The Dean')\n822\t    {\n823\t        $approved = [];\n824\t        $skipped = [];\n825\t\n826\t        foreach ($userIDs as $userID) {\n827\t            $user = User::find($userID);\n828\t            if (!$user || $user->status_id === $statusIds['disapproved'] || $user->status_id !== $statusIds['pending']) {\n829\t                $skipped[] = $userID;\n830\t                continue;\n831\t            }\n832\t\n833\t            $user->update([\n834\t                'status_id' => $statusIds['registered'],\n835\t                'isActive' => true\n836\t            ]);\n837\t\n838\t            // Send email notification\n839\t            $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification($user, 'approved', $approverName);\n840\t\n841\t            $approved[] = $user;\n842\t        }\n843\t\n844\t        return ['approved' => $approved, 'skipped' => $skipped];\n845\t    }\n846\t\n847\t    /**\n848\t     * Process multiple user approvals with scope validation\n849\t     * Filters users based on approver's authority and scope\n850\t     */\n851\t    private function processMultipleApprovalsWithScope($userIDs, $statusIds, $authUser, $approverInfo)\n852\t    {\n853\t        $approved = [];\n854\t        $skipped = [];\n855\t        $scopeRestricted = [];\n856\t\n857\t        foreach ($userIDs as $userID) {\n858\t            $user = User::find($userID);\n859\t            \n860\t            // Check if user exists and is pending\n861\t            if (!$user || $user->status_id !== $statusIds['pending']) {\n862\t                $skipped[] = [\n863\t                    'userID' => $userID,\n864\t                    'reason' => 'User not found or not pending'\n865\t                ];\n866\t                continue;\n867\t            }\n868\t\n869\t            // Check role hierarchy\n870\t            if (!$this->canApproveUser($authUser, $user)) {\n871\t                $skipped[] = [\n872\t                    'userID' => $userID,\n873\t                    'reason' => 'Insufficient role authority'\n874\t                ];\n875\t                continue;\n876\t            }\n877\t\n878\t            // Check scope restrictions\n879\t            if (!$this->canApproveUserInScope($authUser, $user)) {\n880\t                $scopeRestricted[] = [\n881\t                    'userID' => $userID,\n882\t                    'reason' => $this->getScopeRestrictionMessage($authUser)\n883\t                ];\n884\t                continue;\n885\t            }\n886\t\n887\t            // Approve the user\n888\t            $user->update([\n889\t                'status_id' => $statusIds['registered'],\n890\t                'isActive' => true\n891\t            ]);\n892\t\n893\t            // Send email notification with approver info\n894\t            $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification(\n895\t                $user,\n896\t                'approved',\n897\t                $approverInfo['display_name']\n898\t            );\n899\t\n900\t            $approved[] = $user;\n901\t\n902\t            Log::info('User           ]);\n907\t        }\n908\t\n909\t        return [\n910\t            'approved' => $approved,\n911\t            'skipped' => $skipped,\n912\t            'scope_restricted' => 'firstName' => 'sometimes|required|string|max:100',\n920\t            'lastName' => 'sometimes|required|string|max:100',\n921\t            'email' => [\n922\t                'sometimes',\n923\t                'required',\n924\t                'email',\n925\t                Rule::unique('users', 'email')->ignore($user->userID, 'userID')\n926\t            ],\n927\t            'userCode' => [\n928\t                'sometimes',\n929\t                'required',\n930\t                'string',\n931\t                'max:50',\n932\t                Rule::unique('users', 'userCode')->ignore($user->userID, 'userID')\n933\t            ],\n934\t        ]);\n935\t    }\n936\t\n937\t    private function updateUserProfile($user, $validated)\n938\t    {\n939\t        if (!$user instanceof User) {\n940\t            $eloquentUser = User::find($user->userID);\n941\t            if (!$eloquentUser) {\n942\t                throw new \\Exception('User record not found.');\n943\t            }\n944\t            foreach ($validated as $field => $value) {\n945\t                $eloquentUser->$field = $value;\n946\t            }\n947\t            $eloquentUser->save();\n948\t        } else {\n949\t            foreach ($validated as $field => $value) {\n950\t                $user->$field = $value;\n951\t            }\n952\t            $user->save();\n953\t        }\n954\t    }\n955\t\n956\t    private function handleValidationError($e)\n957\t    {\n958\t        $customErrors = [];\n959\t        if (isset($e->errors()['email'])) {\n960\t            $customErrors['email'] = ['The email is already in use by another account.'];\n961\t        }\n962\t        if (isset($e->errors()['userCode'])) {\n963\t            $customErrors['userCode'] = ['The user code is already taken.'];\n964\t        }\n965\t        return response()->json([\n966\t            'message' => 'Validation failed.',\n967\t            'errors' => count($customErrors) ? $customErrors : $e->errors()\n968\t        ], 422);\n969\t    }\n970\t\n971\t    private function getAllowedRoleChanges($roleID)\n972\t    {\n973\t        return match($roleID) {\n974\t            4 => [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], // Dean can change all roles\n975\t            5 => [1, 2, 3],      // Associate Dean can change Program Chair, Instructor, and Student\n976\t            3 => [1, 2],         // Program Chair can change Instructor and Student\n977\t            default => []\n978\t        };\n979\t    }\n980\t\n981\t    private function getApproverDisplayName($user)\n982\t    {\n983\t        $approverName = $user->firstName . ' ' . $user->lastName;\n984\t        \n985\t        // Add role and program information\n986\t        $roleNames = [\n987\t            1 => 'Student',\n988\t            2 => 'Instructor',\n989\t            3 => 'Program Chair',\n990\t            4 => 'Dean',\n991\t            5 => 'Associate Dean'\n992\t        ];\n993\t        \n994\t        $roleName = $roleNames[$user->roleID] ?? 'Administrator';\n995\t        \n996\t        // For Program Chair and Instructor, include program name\n997\t        if (in_array($user->roleID, [2, 3]) && $user->program) {\n998\t            $programName = $user->program->programName ?? '';\n999\t            return \"{$approverName} - {$programName} {$roleName}\";\n1000\t        }\n1001\t        \n1002\t        // For Dean and Associate Dean, just show role\n1003\t        if (in_array($user->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n1004\t            return $roleName;\n1005\t        }\n1006\t        \n1007\t        return $approverName;\n1008\t    }\n1009\t\n1010\t    /**\n1011\t     * Get comprehensive approver information including role, scope, and display name\n1012\t     * Provides transparency about who approved and their authority level\n1013\t     */\n1014\t    private function getApproverInfo($user)\n1015\t    {\n1016\t        $roleNames = [\n1017\t            1 => 'Student',\n1018\t            2 => 'Faculty/Instructor',\n1019\t            3 => 'Program Chair',\n1020\t            4 => 'Dean',\n1021\t            5 => 'Associate Dean'\n1022\t        ];\n1023\t        \n1024\t        $roleName = $roleNames[$user->roleID] ?? 'Administrator';\n1025\t        \n1026\t        $displayName = $user->firstName . ' ' . $user->lastName;\n1027\t        $campusName = $user->campus ? $user->campus->campusName : 'N/A';\n1028\t        $programName = $user->program ? $user->program->programName : 'N/A';\n1029\t        \n1030\t        $info = [\n1031\t            'approver_id' => $user->userID,\n1032\t            'approver_name' => $displayName,\n1033\t            'role' => $roleName,\n1034\t            'campus' => $campusName,\n1035\t            'program' => $programName,\n1036\t            'display_name' => $displayName . ' (' . $roleName . ')',\n1037\t            'scope' => $this->getApproverScope($user)\n1038\t        ];\n1039\t        \n1040\t        // Build detailed display name with scope information\n1041\t        if ($user->roleID === 4) {\n1042\t            $info['display_name'] = $displayName . ' - Dean (Full Authority)';\n1043\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 5) {\n1044\t            $info['display_name'] = $displayName . ' - Associate Dean (' . $campusName . ' Campus)';\n1045\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 3) {\n1046\t            $info['display_name'] = $displayName . ' - Program Chair (' . $programName . ')';\n1047\t        } elseif ($user->roleID === 2) {\n1048\t            $info['display_name'] = $displayName . ' - Faculty (' . $programName . ')';\n1049\t        }\n1050\t        \n1051\t        return $info;\n1052\t    }\n1053\t\n1054\t    /**\n1055\t     * Get the scope of authority for an approver\n1056\t     */\n1057\t    private function getApproverScope($user)\n1058\t    {\n1059\t        switch ($user->roleID) {\n1060\t            case 4:\n1061\t                return 'Institution-wide Authority';\n1062\t            case 5:\n1063\t                return 'Campus: ' . ($user->campus ? $user->campus->campusName : 'Unassigned');\n1064\t            case 3:\n1065\t                return 'Program: ' . ($user->program ? $user->program->programName : 'Unassigned');\n1066\t            case 2:\n1067\t                return 'Students in: ' . ($user->program ? $user->program->programName : 'Unassigned');\n1068\t            default:\n1069\t                return 'Limited Authority';\n1070\t        }\n1071\t    }\n1072\t\n1073\t    /**\n1074\t     * Check if approver can approve user within their assigned scope\n1075\t     * - Program Chairs can ONLY approve within their assigned program\n1076\t     * - Faculty can ONLY approve their own students\n1077\t     * - Deans have no scope restrictions\n1078\t     */\n1079\t    private function canApproveUserInScope($authUser, $targetUser)\n1080\t    {\n1081\t        // Dean has     $baseUrl = rtrim($this->resolveFrontendBaseUrl(), '/');\n253\t        $normalizedPath = '/' . ltrim($path, '/');\n254\t        $query = http_build_query($params);\n255\t\n256\t        return It focuses on actual user-facing filter behavior found in the current mobile frontend and avoids unsupported assumptions.\n5\t\n6\t---\n7\t\n8\t## Scope Note\n9\tThis document covers implemented filtering and search behavior in the mobile application, including:\n10\t- student filters\n11\t- faculty filters\n12\t- program chair filters\n13\t- dean and associate dean filters\n14\t- question review filters\n15\t- leaderboard filters\n16\t\n17\tThis document does not claim that every screen has advanced filtering. It only describes filtering behavior that is clearly present in the current mobile implementation.\n18\t\n19\t---\n20\t\n21\t## Table of Contents\n22\t1. [Overview](#overview)\n23\t2. [Student Filtering Features](#student-filtering-features)\n24\t3. [Faculty Filtering Features](#faculty-filtering-features)\n25\t4. [Program Chair Filtering Features](#program-chair-filtering-features)\n26\t5. [Dean and Associate Dean Filtering Features](#dean-and-associate-dean-filtering-features)\n27\t6. [How Filtering Works in the System](#how-filtering-works-in-the-system)\n28\t7. [Notes](#notes)\n29\t\n30\t---\n31\t\n32\t## Overview\n33\tThe CAPS mobile application uses filtering to help users narrow large sets of information and find relevant records faster. The current mobile implementation includes both simple filters and multi-condition filters depending on the screen.\n34\t\n35\tThe implemented filtering approaches include:\n36\t- search-based filtering\n37\t- chip-based filtering\n38\t- segmented filters\n39\t- tab-based filtering\n40\t- dropdown-based filtering\n41\t- role-based list filtering\n42\t- status-based filtering\n43\t- program and subject filtering\n44\t\n45\tIn many screens, filtering happens locally after data has already been loaded into the app. In some screens, selected filter values are also passed into service requests.\n46\t\n47\t---\n48\t\n49\t## Student Filtering Features\n50\t\n51\t### 1. Practice History Filter\n52\tScreen: student `practice-history`\n53\t\n54\tImplemented filters:\n55\t- `All`\n56\t- `Passed`\n57\t- `Failed`\n58\t\n59\tHow it works:\n60\t- The app loads the student practice history.\n61\t- The selected chip changes the visible list.\n62\t- `Passed` shows entries with scores of `75` and above.\n63\t- `Failed` shows entries with scores below `75`.\n64\t\n65\tPurpose:\n66\t- Helps students quickly review successful and unsuccessful exam attempts.\n67\t\n68\t---\n69\t\n70\t### 2. Subject Search and Category Filter\n71\tScreen: student `search`\n72\t\n73\tImplemented filters:\n74\t- `All`\n75\t- `Program`\n76\t- `General`\n77\t\n78\tImplemented search behavior:\n79\t- search by subject name\n80\t- search by subject code\n81\t\n82\tHow it works:\n83\t- The app loads available practice subjects.\n84\t- The user can search using text input.\n85\t- The user can also apply category chips:\n86\t  - `Program` keeps subjects with a `programID`\n87\t  - `General` keeps subjects without a `programID`\n88\t  - `All` shows the full set\n89\t\n90\tPurpose:\n91\t- Helps students locate practice subjects more quickly and separate program-based subjects from general subjects.\n92\t\n93\t---\n94\t\n95\t### 3. Leaderboard Filter Sheet\n96\tScreen: student `leaderboard`\n97\t\n98\tImplemented filters:\n99\t- period filter:\n100\t  - `Weekly`\n101\t  - `All Time`\n102\t- program filter\n103\t- subject filter\n104\t\n105\tHow it works:\n106\t- The segmented control switches the leaderboard period.\n107\t- A filter sheet opens when the user taps the filter control.\n108\t- Inside the filter sheet, the user can choose:\n109\t  - `All Programs` or a specific program\n110\t  - `All Subjects` or a specific subject\n111\t- The app reloads leaderboard data whenever the selected period, program, or subject changes.\n112\t\n113\tPurpose:\n114\t- Helps students compare rankings in a more focused way, especially by program or subject.\n115\t\n116\t---\n117\t\n118\t### 4. Practice Exam Result Review Filter\n119\tScreen: `practice-exam/results`\n120\t\n121\tImplemented filters:\n122\t- `All Questions`\n123\t- `Correct`\n124\t- `Incorrect`\n125\t\n126\tHow it works:\n127\t- After an exam, the question review section uses tabs to filter the result list.\n128\t- `Correct` shows only correct answers.\n129\t- `Incorrect` shows only incorrect answers.\n130\t- `All Questions` restores the full review list.\n131\t\n132\tPurpose:\n133\t- Helps students review mistakes faster and focus on weak areas.\n134\t\n135\t---\n136\t\n137\t## Faculty Filtering Features\n138\t\n139\t### 1. User Management Filters\n140\tScreen: faculty `users`\n141\t\n142\tImplemented filters:\n143\t- search filter\n144\t- role filter\n145\t- status filter\n146\t- advanced filters:\n147\t  - program\n148\t  - year\n149\t  - campus\n150\t\n151\tRole filter options:\n152\t- `All`\n153\t- `Admins`\n154\t- `Students`\n155\t\n156\tStatus filter options:\n157\t- `All`\n158\t- `Pending`\n159\t- `Active`\n160\t- `Inactive`\n161\t- `Disapproved`\n162\t\n163\tSearch fields used:\n164\t- first name\n165\t- last name\n166\t- email\n167\t- user code\n168\t\n169\tHow it works:\n170\t- The screen loads the full user list.\n171\t- Filtering is then applied locally based on the active selections.\n172\t- The advanced filter panel opens when the options button is tapped.\n173\t- Program, year, and campus use dropdown-style selection.\n174\t\n175\tPurpose:\n176\t- Helps faculty narrow user records without scrolling through the full list.\n177\t\n178\t---\n179\t\n180\t### 2. Class Search Filter\n181\tScreen: faculty `classes`\n182\t\n183\tImplemented filtering behavior:\n184\t- search by class name\n185\t- search by class code\n186\t- search by subject code\n187\t- search by subject name\n188\t- search by schedule\n189\t\n190\tPurpose:\n191\t- Helps faculty quickly find a specific class from the class list.\n192\t\n193\t---\n194\t\n195\t## Program Chair Filtering Features\n196\t\n197\t### 1. User Management Filters\n198\tScreen: program chair `users`\n199\t\n200\tImplemented filters:\n201\t- search filter\n202\t- role filter\n203\t- status filter\n204\t- advanced filters:\n205\t  - program\n206\t  - year\n207\t  - campus\n208\t\n209\tThis works in the same general way as the faculty and dean user management filters, with local filtering applied to the loaded user list.\n210\t\n211\tPurpose:\n212\t- Helps program chairs focus on users relevant to the selected category or academic grouping.\n213\t\n214\t---\n215\t\n216\t### 2. Class Search Filter\n217\tScreen: program chair `classes`\n218\t\n219\tImplemented filtering behavior:\n220\t- search by class name\n221\t- search by class code\n222\t- search by subject code\n223\t- search by subject name\n224\t- search by schedule\n225\t\n226\tPurpose:\n227\t- Helps program chairs quickly locate relevant classes in the mobile class overview.\n228\t\n229\t---\n230\t\n231\t## Dean and Associate Dean Filtering Features\n232\t\n233\t### 1. User Management Filters\n234\tScreens:\n235\t- dean `users`\n236\t- associate dean `users`\n237\t\n238\tImplemented filters:\n239\t- search filter\n240\t- role filter\n241\t- status filter\n242\t- advanced filters:\n243\t  - program\n244\t  - year\n245\t  - campus\n246\t\n247\tRole filter options:\n248\t- `All`\n249\t- `Admins`\n250\t- `Students`\n251\t\n252\tStatus filter options:\n253\t- `All`\n254\t- `Pending`\n255\t- `Active`\n256\t- `Inactive`\n257\t- `Disapproved`\n258\t\n259\tAdvanced dropdown filters:\n260\t- `Program`\n261\t- `Year`\n262\t- `Campus`\n263\t\n264\tSearch fields used:\n265\t- first name\n266\t- last name\n267\t- email\n268\t- user code\n269\t\n270\tAdditional behavior:\n271\t- dashboard shortcuts can open the users screen with a preselected role filter in some cases\n272\t\n273\tPurpose:\n274\t- Helps administrators narrow large user lists and review specific user groups faster.\n275\t\n276\t---\n277\t\n278\t### 2. Class Search Filter\n279\tScreens:\n280\t- dean `classes`\n281\t- associate dean `classes`\n282\t\n283\tImplemented filtering behavior:\n284\t- search by class name\n285\t- search by class code\n286\t- search by subject code\n287\t- search by subject name\n288\t- search by schedule\n289\t\n290\tPurpose:\n291\t- Helps administrators quickly locate classes in the oversight workflow.\n292\t\n293\t---\n294\t\n295\t### 3. Question Search Filter\n296\tScreen: dean `subjects`\n297\t\n298\tImplemented filtering behavior:\n299\t- search by question text\n300\t- search by topic\n301\t- search by subject name\n302\t\n303\tHow it works:\n304\t- Questions are loaded for the selected subject.\n305\t- The search input narrows the visible question list.\n306\t- The current implementation is search-based and does not expose a separate status chip filter in the main list.\n307\t\n308\tPurpose:\n309\t- Helps administrators find specific questions in the subject question bank more quickly.\n310\t\n311\t---\n312\t\n313\t### 4. Unified Enhancement Student Filters\n314\tScreen: `AdminUnifiedEnhancementScreen`\n315\t\n316\tImplemented filters:\n317\t- program filter\n318\t- student search filter\n319\t\n320\tHow it works:\n321\t- The screen loads student and analytics data.\n322\t- Program filtering narrows students by normalized program value.\n323\t- Search filtering matches student name and email.\n324\t- Both conditions are applied together to produce the visible student list.\n325\t\n326\tPurpose:\n327\t- Helps deans and associate deans review student data with a more focused student subset.\n328\t\n329\tNote:\n330\t- The current file includes a comment that performance-based filters are limited because per-student score filtering is not fully available from the current backend data.\n331\t\n332\t---\n333\t\n334\t## How Filtering Works in the System\n335\t\n336\t### Local filtering\n337\tMany mobile screens load a dataset first, then filter the visible records in memory using:\n338\t- active chip values\n339\t- selected dropdown values\n340\t- search text\n341\t- tab state\n342\t\n343\tThis is common in:\n344\t- user management screens\n345\t- practice history\n346\t- subject search\n347\t- question review\n348\t- class lists\n349\t\n350\t### Request-driven filtering\n351\tSome filters affect the service request itself.\n352\t\n353\tThis is clearly present in:\n354\t- student leaderboard, where period, program, and subject selections are passed into the leaderboard service request\n355\t\n356\t### Combined filtering\n357\tSome screens use multiple filters together.\n358\t\n359\tExamples:\n360\t- user management combines search, role, status, program, year, and campus\n361\t- student leaderboard combines period, program, and subject\n362\t- unified enhancement combines program and search\n363\t\n364\t---\n365\t\n366\t## Notes\n367\t- This document was written to reflect currently implemented mobile filtering behavior.\n368\t- Search and filtering are not identical on every screen. Some screens use only search, while others use several filters together.\n369\t- Several filters are role-specific and appear only in administrative or faculty workflows.\n370\t- If new chips, dropdowns, analytics filters, or backend-driven filtering options are added later, this document should be updated.\n371\t\n372\t---\n373\t\n374\t*End of Document*\n375\t\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. successfully.',\n174\t            'activated_users' => $activatedUsers\n175\t        ], 200);\n176\t    }\n177\t\n178\t    /**\n179\t     * Deactivate multiple users (Only Dean can do this).\n180\t     */\n181\t    public function deactivateMultipleUsers(Request $request)\n182\t    {\n183\t        $this->authorizeDeanAccess();\n184\t        $validated = $this->validateUserIDs($request);\n185\t        \n186\t        $deactivatedUsers = $this->processMultipleUsers($validated['userIDs'], false);\n187\t\n188\t        return response()->json([\n189\t            'message' => 'Selected users hierarchy:\n196\t     * - Dean (4) can approve all roles\n197\t     * - Associate Dean (5) can approve Program Chair (3), Instructor (2), and Student (1) within their campus\n198\t     * - Program Chair (3) can approve Instructor (2) and Student (1) ONLY within their assigned program\n199\t     * - Faculty (2) can approve Student (1) ONLY for their own students\n200\t     */\n201\t    public function approveUser(Request $request, $userID)\n202\t    {\n203\t        try {\n204\t            $authUser = Auth::user();\n205\t            $user = User::findOrFail($userID);\n206\t\n207\t            // Validate if user has permission to approve\n208\t            if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [2, 3, 4, 5])) {\n209\t                return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized. You do not have permission to approve users.'], 403);\n210\t            }\n211\t\n212\t            // Check if user is pending\n213\t            if (!$this->isUserPending($user)) {\n214\t                return response()->json(['message' => 'User is already registered.'], 400);\n215\t            }\n216\t\n217\t            // Validate role hierarchy for approval\n218\t            if (!$this->canApproveUser($authUser, $user)) {\n219\t                return response()->json([\n220\t                    'message' => 'You are not authorized to approve users with this role level.'\n221\t                ], 403);\n222\t            }\n223\t\n224\t            // Validate scope-based restrictions\n225\t            if (!$this->canApproveUserInScope($authUser, $user)) {\n226\t                return response()->json([\n227\t                    'message' => 'You cannot approve users outside your assigned scope. ' .\n228\t                                $this->getScopeRestrictionMessage($authUser)\n229\t                ], 403);\n230\t            }\n231\t\n232\t            $this->updateUserStatus($user, 'registered', true);\n233\t\n234\t            $approverInfo = $this->getApproverInfo($authUser);\n235\t            $emailSent = $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification($user, 'approved', $approverInfo['display_name']);\n236\t            if (!$emailSent) {\n237\t                Log::warning('Approval email failed to send', [\n238\t                    'user_id' => $user->userID,\n239\t                    'email' => $user->email,\n240\t                    'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n241\t                    'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n242\t                ]);\n243\t\n244\t                return response()->json([\n245\t                    'message' => 'User approved, but approval email failed to send.',\n246\t                    'user' => $user,\n247\t                    'email' => $user->email,\n248\t                    'approval_info' => $approverInfo,\n249\t                ], 200);\n250\t            }\n251\t\n252\t            Log::info('User approved successfully', [\n253\t                'user_id' => $user->userID,\n254\t                'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n255\t                'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n256\t                'approver_scope' => $approverInfo['scope'],\n257\t            ]);\n258\t\n259\t            return response()->json([\n260\t                'message' => 'User approved successfully.',\n261\t                'user' => $user,\n262\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n263\t            ], 200);\n264\t\n265\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n266\t            Log::error('User approval error: ' . $e->getMessage());\n267\t            return response()->json([\n268\t                'message' => 'An error occurred while approving the user.',\n269\t                'error' => $e->getMessage()\n270\t            ], 500);\n271\t        }\n272\t    }\n273\t\n274\t    /**\n275\t     * Check if the authenticated user can approve the target user based on role hierarchy\n276\t     */\n277\t    private function canApproveUser($authUser, $targetUser)\n278\t    {\n279\t        // Dean can approve anyone\n280\t        if ($authUser->roleID === 4) {\n281\t            return true;\n282\t        }\n283\t\n284\t        // Get allowed roles for approval based on auth user's role\n285\t        $allowedRoles = $this->getAllowedApprovalRoles($authUser->roleID);\n286\t\n287\t        // Check if target user's role is in the allowed roles\n288\t        return in_array($targetUser->roleID, $allowedRoles);\n289\t    }\n290\t\n291\t    /**\n292\t     * Get the list of roles that can be approved by a given role\n293\t     */\n294\t    private function getAllowedApprovalRoles($roleID)\n295\t    {\n296\t        return match($roleID) {\n297\t            4 => [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], // Dean can approve all\n298\t            5 => [1, 2, 3],      // Associate Dean can approve Program Chair, Instructor, and Student\n299\t            3 => [1, 2],         // Program Chair can approve Instructor and Student\n300\t            2 => [1],            // Faculty can approve Student\n301\t            default => []\n302\t        };\n303\t    }\n304\t\n305\t    /**\n306\t     * Disapprove user (Only Dean and Associate Dean).\n307\t     * Scope restriction: Associate Deans can only disapprove within their campus,\n308\t     * but Program Chairs and Faculty cannot disapprove (only Dean/Associate Dean can).\n309\t     */\n310\t    public function disapproveUser(Request $request, $userID)\n311\t    {\n312\t        $authUser = Auth::user();\n313\t        \n314\t        // Only Dean (4) and Associate Dean (5) can disapprove\n315\t        if (!in_array($authUser->roleID, [4, 5])) {\n316\t            return response()->json(['message' => 'Unauthorized: Only Dean or Associate Dean can disapprove users.'], 403);\n317\t        }\n318\t\n319\t        $user = User::findOrFail($userID);\n320\t\n321\t        // Associate Dean scope check: can only disapprove users in their campus\n322\t        if ($authUser->roleID === 5 && $user->campusID !== $authUser->campusID) {\n323\t            return $this->updateUserStatus($user, 'disapproved', false);\n329\t\n330\t        $approverInfo = $this->getApproverInfo($authUser);\n331\t        $emailSent = $this->emailService->sendStatusNotification($user, 'disapproved', $approverInfo['display_name']);\n332\t        if (!$emailSent) {\n333\t            Log::warning('Disapproval email failed to send', [\n334\t                'user_id' => $user->userID,\n335\t                'email' => $user->email,\n336\t                'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n337\t                'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n338\t            ]);\n339\t\n340\t            return response()->json([\n341\t                'message' => 'User disapproved, but disapproval email failed to send.',\n342\t                'user' => $user,\n343\t                'email' => $user->email,\n344\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo,\n345\t            ], 200);\n346\t        }\n347\t\n348\t        Log::info('User disapproved', [\n349\t            'user_id' => $user->userID,\n350\t            'approver_id' => $authUser->userID,\n351\t            'approver_role' => $approverInfo['role'],\n352\t        ]);\n353\t\n354\t        return response()->json([\n355\t            'message' => 'User has been disapproved.',\n356\t            'user' => $user,\n357\t            'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n358\t        ], 200);\n359\t    }\n360\t\n361\t    /**\n362\t     * Approve multiple users at once with role-based scope validation.\n363\t     * Dean can approve all, Associate Dean within their campus,\n364\t     * Program Chair within their program, Faculty for their students.\n365\t     */\n366\t    public function approveMultipleUsers(Request $request)\n367\t    {\n368\t          $statusIds,\n385\t                $authUser,\n386\t                $approverInfo\n387\t            );\n388\t\n389\t            return response()->json([\n390\t                'message' => => $results['scope_restricted'],\n394\t                'approval_info' => $approverInfo\n395\t            ], 200);\n396\t        } catch (\\Exception $e) {\n397\t            Log::error('Bulk approval $e->getMessage()\n401\t            ], 500);\n402\t        }\n403\t    }\n404\t\n405\t    /**\n406\t     * Update the authenticated user's profile.\n407\t     */\n408\t    public function
